working_definition: A group 2 innate lymphoid cell present in prenatal human skin from early gestation, characterized by expression of the transcription factor GATA3 and markers IL1RL1, IL7R, and TNFRSF18. These cells are tissue-resident innate lymphocytes capable of producing type 2 cytokines (IL-5, IL-13) in response to epithelial alarmins such as IL-33 and TSLP, contributing to tissue homeostasis, repair, and immune regulation in developing skin.
searches_performed: OLS4 searchClasses: 'group 2 innate lymphoid cell' in CL ontology
snippet_search: 'ILC2 innate lymphoid cell type 2 prenatal skin' scoped to atlas DOI:10.1038/s41586-024-08002-x
snippet_search: 'ILC2 group 2 innate lymphoid cell fetal prenatal skin GATA3 development' broad search

best_match.cl_definition: An innate lymphoid cell that is capable of producing T-helper 2-cell associated cytokines upon stimulation.

justification: The ILC2 population identified in the prenatal skin atlas (annotated as ILC2_3) expresses canonical ILC2 markers GATA3, IL1RL1, IL7R, and TNFRSF18, which are defining features of group 2 innate lymphoid cells. The CL:0001069 definition describes an innate lymphoid cell capable of producing Th2-associated cytokines, which matches the atlas annotation and literature characterization of these cells. The prenatal developmental context does not alter the fundamental cell type identity.
recommendation: Map to CL:0001069 (group 2 innate lymphoid cell). No new CL term is needed.
